event_text: William Duke of Normandy defeats King Harold II at the Battle of Hastings.
William I is crowned King of England at Westminster Abbey.
The Domesday survey records landholding and resources across much of England.
Thomas Becket is murdered in Canterbury Cathedral.
King John seals Magna Carta at Runnymede.
Simon de Montfort's parliament includes elected representatives from towns and shires.
Edward I conquers Wales after the death of Llywelyn ap Gruffudd.
Edward of Caernarfon becomes Prince of Wales.
Robert the Bruce defeats Edward II at the Battle of Bannockburn.
The Black Death reaches England and causes severe population loss.
The Peasants' Revolt challenges taxation and social inequality in England.
The Wars of the Roses begin between the houses of Lancaster and York.
Henry Tudor defeats Richard III at Bosworth and becomes Henry VII.
John Cabot sails from Bristol and reaches North America.
The Protestant Reformation begins in Europe and later reshapes religion in Britain.
The Act of Supremacy makes Henry VIII Supreme Head of the Church of England.
The Laws in Wales Acts begin the legal union of Wales and England.
The dissolution of the monasteries begins under Henry VIII.
The first Book of Common Prayer is introduced in England.
Elizabeth I becomes Queen of England.
The Spanish Armada is defeated.
James VI of Scotland becomes James I of England and unites the crowns.
The Gunpowder Plot to blow up Parliament is uncovered.
The King James Bible is published.
The Mayflower departs Plymouth carrying English colonists to North America.
The English Civil War begins when Charles I raises his standard at Nottingham.
Charles I is executed outside the Banqueting House in London.
Oliver Cromwell becomes Lord Protector.
The monarchy is restored and Charles II enters London.
The Great Plague kills thousands in London.
The Great Fire of London begins in Pudding Lane.
The Glorious Revolution removes James II and brings William and Mary to the throne.
The Bill of Rights receives royal assent and limits royal power.
The Acts of Union create the Kingdom of Great Britain.
A Jacobite rising attempts to restore the Stuart line.
The Jacobite rising led by Charles Edward Stuart begins.
The Battle of Culloden ends the Jacobite rising.
Britain adopts the Gregorian calendar.
The Seven Years' War begins and expands British imperial conflict.
James Hargreaves invents the spinning jenny, accelerating textile production.
Richard Arkwright patents the water frame and helps transform cotton manufacturing.
British forces and American colonists clash at Lexington and Concord.
The American colonies declare independence from Great Britain.
The world's first iron bridge opens at Coalbrookdale in Shropshire.
The Gordon Riots erupt in London.
The First Fleet reaches Sydney Cove and begins British settlement in Australia.
The French Revolution begins and affects politics and reform movements in Britain.
Britain enters the wars against Revolutionary France.
The Acts of Union bring Great Britain and Ireland together as the United Kingdom.
Nelson's victory at Trafalgar secures British naval dominance.
The Slave Trade Act abolishes the British slave trade.
Luddite protests begin against industrial machinery and working conditions.
British and allied forces defeat Napoleon at Waterloo.
The Peterloo Massacre takes place in Manchester.
The Stockton and Darlington Railway opens as the world's first public steam railway.
The Catholic Relief Act allows most Catholics to sit in Parliament.
The Great Reform Act receives royal assent and changes parliamentary representation.
The Slavery Abolition Act receives royal assent.
The Poor Law Amendment Act reshapes poor relief in England and Wales.
Queen Victoria comes to the throne.
The first People's Charter is published by the Chartist movement.
The Penny Black postage stamp is issued.
The Mines and Collieries Act restricts women and children working underground.
The Great Famine begins in Ireland after potato blight devastates crops.
The Corn Laws are repealed after years of political pressure over food prices.
The Great Exhibition opens at the Crystal Palace in London.
Britain enters the Crimean War.
The Indian Rebellion begins against East India Company rule.
The Government of India Act transfers rule in India from the East India Company to the Crown.
The Metropolitan Railway opens in London, becoming the world's first underground passenger railway.
The Second Reform Act expands the male electorate in England and Wales.
The Elementary Education Act lays foundations for state schooling in England and Wales.
The Ballot Act introduces secret voting in parliamentary elections.
The Third Reform Act extends the vote to many more working men.
The Local Government Act creates elected county councils in England and Wales.
The Independent Labour Party is founded.
The Second Boer War begins in South Africa.
Queen Victoria dies and Edward VII becomes king.
A Liberal government begins welfare reforms including school meals and labour exchanges.
Old age pensions are introduced in the United Kingdom.
George V becomes king after the death of Edward VII.
The Parliament Act limits the power of the House of Lords.
RMS Titanic sinks in the North Atlantic with heavy loss of life.
Britain enters the First World War.
RMS Lusitania is sunk by a German submarine.
The Battle of the Somme begins.
The Easter Rising begins in Dublin.
The Representation of the People Act gives some women and all qualifying men the parliamentary vote.
The Armistice ends fighting in the First World War.
Nancy Astor becomes the first woman to take her seat as a Member of Parliament.
The Anglo-Irish Treaty is signed.
The British Broadcasting Company is founded.
The General Strike begins.
The Equal Franchise Act gives women voting rights on the same terms as men.
The Wall Street Crash triggers global economic depression that affects Britain.
Edward VIII abdicates and George VI becomes king.
George VI is crowned at Westminster Abbey.
Britain declares war on Germany and enters the Second World War.
Winston Churchill becomes Prime Minister.
The Dunkirk evacuation begins, rescuing Allied troops from northern France.
The Battle of Britain begins over the skies of southern England and the Channel.
The Blitz begins with major German air raids on London.
D-Day landings begin the liberation of western Europe.
VE Day marks the end of the war in Europe.
Labour wins the general election and Clement Attlee becomes Prime Minister.
VJ Day marks Japan's surrender and the end of the Second World War.
The National Health Service is launched.
The Empire Windrush arrives at Tilbury, symbolising post-war Caribbean migration to Britain.
The United Kingdom becomes a founding member of NATO.
Elizabeth II becomes Queen after the death of George VI.
Queen Elizabeth II is crowned at Westminster Abbey.
The Suez Crisis exposes Britain's reduced imperial power.
The Wolfenden Report recommends decriminalising private homosexual acts between men.
The first modern contraceptive pill is licensed in Britain.
The Profumo affair damages the Conservative government.
The Race Relations Act becomes the first UK law to address racial discrimination.
The Aberfan disaster kills 116 children and 28 adults in Wales.
The Sexual Offences Act partially decriminalises homosexual acts between men in England and Wales.
The Abortion Act receives royal assent.
The voting age is lowered from 21 to 18.
Britain changes to decimal currency.
Bloody Sunday occurs in Derry during a civil rights march.
The United Kingdom joins the European Economic Community.
The Three-Day Week ends after an energy crisis and industrial action.
The UK votes to remain in the European Community in a national referendum.
Louise Brown, the first baby born through IVF, is born in Oldham.
Margaret Thatcher becomes the UK's first female Prime Minister.
Prince Charles and Lady Diana Spencer marry at St Paul's Cathedral.
Argentine forces invade the Falkland Islands, beginning the Falklands War.
The UK miners' strike begins and becomes one of Britain's major industrial disputes.
Live Aid concerts are held in London and Philadelphia.
A fire at King's Cross Underground station kills 31 people.
Pan Am Flight 103 is destroyed over Lockerbie in Scotland.
Poll Tax protests in London turn violent.
Margaret Thatcher resigns and John Major becomes Prime Minister.
The Channel Tunnel is formally opened.
Labour wins the general election and Tony Blair becomes Prime Minister.
Diana Princess of Wales dies in a car crash in Paris.
Scotland votes in favour of a devolved parliament.
Wales votes narrowly in favour of devolution.
The Good Friday Agreement is reached in Northern Ireland.
The Scottish Parliament is formally opened.
The National Assembly for Wales takes on devolved powers.
The terrorist attacks in the United States reshape UK security and foreign policy.
UK forces join the invasion of Iraq.
Coordinated terrorist bombings on London's transport system kill 52 people.
A smoking ban comes into force in enclosed public places in England.
The global financial crisis leads to bank rescues and recession in the UK.
David Cameron becomes Prime Minister and forms a coalition government with the Liberal Democrats.
The London Olympic Games open.
The Marriage Same Sex Couples Act receives royal assent in England and Wales.
Scotland votes to remain in the United Kingdom in an independence referendum.
The UK votes to leave the European Union in a national referendum.
The Grenfell Tower fire kills 72 people in London.
The UK enters its first national COVID-19 lockdown.
The United Kingdom formally leaves the European Union.
Queen Elizabeth II dies at Balmoral and Charles III becomes king.
King Charles III and Queen Camilla are crowned at Westminster Abbey.
Labour wins the UK general election and Keir Starmer becomes Prime Minister.
